case.title: OAI showcase case: bilateral knee osteoarthritis with side-specific planning
case.one_line: 67-year-old man with bilateral knee OA: left knee KL4, pain NRS 8 and WOMAC-function burden 62; right knee KL2, pain NRS 4 and milder function burden. KOM-Profile, KOM-Rad and KOM-Risk must retain left and right knees separately; treatment planning prioritizes the left knee while the right knee remains an active monitoring and prevention target.

anchors.Planning scope: Both knees; left knee currently drives escalation, right knee requires monitoring
anchors.Goal: Walk 3 km safely, avoid repeated injections, and understand whether orthopedic specialist evaluation should be recommended

missing: Left and right weight-bearing radiographs with alignment review
Left and right symptom/function anchors at the same visit

standard_cases.title: Early education and monitoring case
standard_cases.purpose: Early bilateral OA with low pain, locked KL1/KL1, low risk and a prevention-focused education plus home exercise plan.

standard_cases.title: Activity-goal rehabilitation case
standard_cases.purpose: Mild left-predominant OA with a return-to-walking/cycling goal; treatment emphasizes FITT-VP progression and low-impact aerobic choices.

standard_cases.title: Comorbidity and safety-gated conservative case
standard_cases.purpose: Symptomatic OA with obesity, CKD/GI/anticoagulant risks, mild anxiety/depression and fall signal; treatment is safety-gated and supervised.

standard_cases.title: Orthopedic referral-screen and prehabilitation case
standard_cases.purpose: Advanced left knee OA with locked KL4/KL2, high symptoms and walking goal; treatment adds referral screening, prehabilitation and missing-data warnings.

architecture.title: KOM-Assess patient assessment subsystem
architecture.summary: Builds side-specific left-knee and right-knee profiles, structured imaging interpretation and longitudinal risk prediction before treatment planning.

modules.name: KOM-Profile
modules.detail: Editable demographics plus left and right knee KL grade, pain, WOMAC/KOOS, strength, balance, medication safety, prior treatment and patient goals.

modules.name: KOM-Rad
modules.detail: Shows original bilateral OAI radiographs first, then reveals left-knee and right-knee KL / JSN / osteophyte / sclerosis findings after running interpretation.

modules.name: KOM-Risk
modules.detail: Displays structural progression, TKA-event and symptom/function worsening risk separately for left and right knees.

architecture.title: KOM-Treat MDT prescription subsystem
architecture.summary: Routes the bilateral profile into the evidence graph, specialty agents, safety negotiation and structured clinical report.

modules.name: KOM-RAG
modules.detail: Retrieves guideline, meta-analysis, RCT and contextual evidence from the Evidence Unit database.

modules.name: KOM-MDT
modules.detail: Exercise, nutrition, medication/injection, psychology and orthopedic boundary agents generate specialty prescriptions.

modules.name: KOM-Safe
modules.detail: Routes failed gates back to the responsible specialty agent and records revision, re-audit and adoption status.

modules.name: KOM-Rx
modules.detail: Curates patient assessment, MDT prescription, reasoning trail and clinician-review priorities.

modules.name: KOM-Score
modules.detail: Separates rule/model validation, expert prescription quality review and safety-event auditing.

fields.label: Age
fields.help: Chronological age; affects sarcopenia risk, surgical preparation and medication safety.

fields.label: Sex
fields.help: Used for clinical context and risk interpretation.

fields.label: BMI
fields.help: Body mass index; supports weight-management and risk simulation.

fields.label: Planning scope
fields.help: Use Both knees for routine KOM assessment. Left and right knee predictions remain separate even when one side drives escalation.

fields.label: Left KL grade
fields.help: Kellgren-Lawrence grade for the left knee: 0 none, 1 doubtful, 2 definite osteophytes, 3 definite joint-space narrowing, 4 severe joint-space narrowing with advanced osteophyte/sclerosis.

fields.label: Right KL grade
fields.help: Kellgren-Lawrence grade for the right knee, used for side-to-side comparison and monitoring.

fields.label: Left knee pain NRS
fields.help: Left knee 0-10 pain score at the current visit; used in left-knee KOM-Risk.

fields.label: Right knee pain NRS
fields.help: Right knee 0-10 pain score at the current visit; used in right-knee KOM-Risk.

fields.label: Left WOMAC function
fields.help: Left-knee function burden when side-specific scoring is available; higher is worse.

fields.label: Right WOMAC function
fields.help: Right-knee function burden when side-specific scoring is available; higher is worse.

fields.label: Left progression status
fields.help: Clinician-recorded left-knee structural or clinical progression state.

fields.label: Right progression status
fields.help: Clinician-recorded right-knee structural or clinical progression state.

fields.label: Overall pain NRS
fields.help: Overall 0-10 pain anchor used only when a side-specific pain score is missing.

fields.label: WOMAC pain
fields.help: WOMAC pain subscore; can be calculated from item-level responses.

fields.label: WOMAC stiffness
fields.help: WOMAC stiffness subscore; can be calculated from item-level responses.

fields.label: Overall WOMAC function
fields.help: Overall WOMAC function fallback; side-specific function values should be entered when available.

fields.label: KOOS-ADL
fields.help: KOOS activities-of-daily-living score from 0 to 100; higher is better.

fields.label: Left quadriceps strength
fields.value: MRC 4-/5; isometric knee-extension peak force not measured
fields.help: Record left quadriceps MRC grade and, if measured, OAI-style isometric knee-extension peak force.

fields.label: Right quadriceps strength
fields.value: MRC 4+/5; isometric knee-extension peak force not measured
fields.help: Record right quadriceps MRC grade and numeric knee-extension strength if measured.

fields.label: Balance status
options: Single-leg stance <10 s or needs support
fields.value: Single-leg stance <10 s or needs support
fields.help: Balance decline triggers fall-prevention rehabilitation.

fields.label: eGFR / creatinine
fields.help: Required before routine oral NSAID use and before fixed high-protein targets.

fields.label: GI ulcer / bleeding
fields.help: Determines oral NSAID gastrointestinal risk.

fields.label: Anticoagulant / antiplatelet status
fields.help: Affects NSAID, injection and perioperative safety.

fields.label: Cardiovascular risk
fields.help: Used when deciding whether oral NSAID is appropriate.

fields.label: Current medication list
fields.help: Medication reconciliation focused on NSAID, injection and perioperative risk; choose explicit none if no key risk medication is present.

fields.label: Conservative-treatment history
fields.help: Record education, self-management, rehabilitation, weight management, topical/oral medication, bracing and previous injections.

fields.label: Quality-of-life goal
fields.value: Walk 3 km safely, avoid repeated injections, and discuss whether specialist referral should be considered
fields.help: Patient-centered treatment goal, such as walking distance, work, sport, sleep, stairs or injection preference.

fields.label: Avoid repeated injections
fields.help: Downgrades routine injection and supports bridge-only injection language.

fields.label: Referral question
fields.help: Whether the patient wants to know if orthopedic evaluation should be discussed.

fields.label: Exercise preference / access
fields.value: Walking goal with supervised prehabilitation; cycling acceptable
fields.help: Patient-preferred modalities and practical access. Used to choose walking, cycling, aquatic, resistance or balance modules.

fields.label: GAD-7 anxiety signal
fields.help: Brief anxiety screen. Positive or moderate signals change communication and referral thresholds.

fields.label: PHQ-9 depression signal
fields.help: Brief depression screen. Item 9 or severe symptoms require urgent mental-health boundary review.

fields.label: Sleep quality / insomnia
fields.help: Sleep disturbance may amplify pain and reduce rehabilitation adherence.

fields.label: Pain catastrophizing signal
fields.help: Records fear, catastrophizing or avoidance signal for CBT-informed explanation and pacing.

fields.label: Nutrition / metabolic risk
fields.value: BMI elevated; weight-loss target must preserve muscle
fields.help: Flags obesity, renal disease, malnutrition risk or metabolic issues before nutrition targets are prescribed.

fields.label: Cardiovascular pre-referral screen
fields.value: Self-reported exertional dyspnea not yet characterized
fields.help: Initial patient-reported CV screen before orthopedic referral. This is not a full cardiology clearance.

fields.label: Respiratory pre-referral screen
fields.value: Former smoker; no spirometry report collected
fields.help: Initial patient-reported respiratory screen before referral. Full testing belongs to surgical workup if admitted.

fields.label: Skin / dental infection screen
fields.value: Skin intact; dental status not recorded
fields.help: Initial infection-risk screen before referral discussion.

fields.label: Weight-bearing alignment imaging
fields.value: Weight-bearing AP/lateral radiographs present; long-leg alignment not collected
fields.help: Basic imaging status for referral screening; KL is fixed from this profile once entered.

fields.label: Knee ROM / contracture
fields.help: Range of motion and flexion contracture screening for prehabilitation and referral readiness.

followup.collect: Left and right KL/JSN/osteophyte/sclerosis, side-specific NRS, WOMAC/KOOS, strength, fall risk, medication safety and prior treatment by knee
followup.decision: Frame bilateral burden, primary planning knee, monitoring knee and initial MDT plan.

followup.collect: Left/right pain NRS, function, skin reaction, adherence, flare/effusion, exercise tolerance and walking distance
followup.decision: Adjust topical/oral medication boundary and rehabilitation progression separately for symptomatic and monitoring knees.

followup.collect: Left/right WOMAC/KOOS change, walking tolerance, quadriceps strength, falls, weight trend and adverse effects
followup.decision: Recommend orthopedic specialist evaluation if severe side-specific limitation persists despite structured conservative care.

followup.collect: Weight, waist, metabolic profile, strength, side-specific symptom trajectory and updated imaging when clinically indicated
followup.decision: Review long-term MDT plan, referral readiness, preoperative warning status and patient goals.

imaging.model_note: Embedded OAK-Net / KOM-Rad structured interpretation module. Outputs are side-specific for left and right knees and require clinician review; they are not an autonomous diagnosis.

examples.medial_jsn: Severe medial joint-space narrowing, compartment grade 3
examples.osteophyte: Severe marginal osteophyte formation, OARSI osteophyte grade 3
examples.sclerosis: Definite subchondral sclerosis in the medial compartment
examples.review: Severe side-specific structural burden; drives escalation and preoperative warning review.

examples.medial_jsn: Mild to moderate medial joint-space narrowing, compartment grade 1-2
examples.osteophyte: Mild marginal osteophyte formation, OARSI osteophyte grade 1-2
examples.review: Milder OA; still receives risk prediction, prevention, strengthening and follow-up monitoring.

risk.name: Structural progression risk
risk.definition: KL0-3: KL progression >=1 grade or joint-space narrowing worsening; KL4: end-stage structural ceiling rather than KL+1 progression
risk.meaning: Higher values indicate greater probability of measurable structural worsening in KL0-3 knees. KL4 is handled as a radiographic ceiling state, not a claim that a KL4 knee can progress to KL5.

risk.name: TKA-event risk
risk.meaning: Higher values support earlier orthopedic specialist evaluation and preoperative warning review for the affected knee.

risk.name: Symptom/function worsening risk
risk.definition: Clinically meaningful worsening in WOMAC pain or function
risk.meaning: Higher values indicate need for closer side-specific follow-up, adherence support and rehabilitation adjustment.

level_definitions: Direct recommendation anchor when current and in scope.
Evidence synthesis supporting effect, safety or comparative options.
Trial-level calibration for dose, delivery or expected response.
Care delivery, adherence and implementation context.
Background only unless supported by higher levels.
Context only; not a direct prescription anchor.

exercise_rehabilitation.title: Exercise and rehabilitation evidence chain
exercise_rehabilitation.clinical_question: For bilateral knee OA with fixed left/right KL grades, symptoms, fall-risk or walking goals, which exercise modules should be selected by guideline core evidence, meta-analysis signal, RCT signal and patient-fit safety?

items.title: EULAR recommendations for non-pharmacological core management of hip and knee OA: 2023 update
items.summary: Guideline anchor for education, therapeutic exercise, weight management and self-management; use as boundary, not as a one-size-fits-all prescription.
items.why_selected: Current European non-pharmacological guideline anchor relevant to exercise and self-management.
items.P_Population: Adults with hip or knee osteoarthritis managed non-pharmacologically.
items.I_Intervention: Education, exercise, self-management, weight management and assistive strategies.
items.C_Comparator: Guideline consensus process; no single trial comparator.
items.O_Outcomes: Core care recommendation; positive consensus for exercise and self-management.
items.Effect_Summary: Guideline-level recommendation without a pooled effect size; use to define standard-of-care boundaries.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; level=L1; use=Direct anchor.

items.title: 2019 ACR/Arthritis Foundation guideline for OA management
items.summary: Strong recommendation for exercise and weight loss in overweight/obese knee OA; conditional psychosocial and mind-body options are retained for patient-fit.
items.why_selected: International guideline anchor used to prevent overclaiming from lower-level exercise studies.
items.P_Population: Adults with hand, hip or knee osteoarthritis; knee OA recommendations are applicable to this workbench.
items.I_Intervention: Exercise, weight loss when indicated, topical/oral NSAID boundaries, self-management, tai chi and selected behavioral options.
items.O_Outcomes: Guideline recommendation strength; exercise is a strong recommendation for knee OA.
items.Effect_Summary: Guideline-level recommendation; no single pooled effect size, so use as treatment boundary rather than magnitude claim.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; level=L1; use=Direct anchor.

items.title: Comparative efficacy and safety of exercise modalities in knee osteoarthritis: systematic review and network meta-analysis
items.summary: 217 RCTs and 15,684 participants; aerobic exercise showed the highest overall probability of benefit across pain, function, gait and QoL.
items.why_selected: Adds result-tier signal for prioritizing aerobic modules before choosing walking, cycling or other low-impact delivery.
items.P_Population: 217 randomized controlled trials including 15,684 adults with knee osteoarthritis; interventions included aerobic, flexibility, mind-body, neuromotor, strengthening and mixed exercise; databases searched to August 2024.
items.I_Intervention: Network meta-analysis comparing aerobic exercise, flexibility exercise, mind-body exercise, neuromotor exercise, strengthening exercise and mixed exercise.
items.C_Comparator: Control group, usual care or another exercise modality across included RCTs.
items.O_Outcomes: Pain, function, gait performance and quality of life at short term (~4 weeks), mid-term (~12 weeks) and long term (~24 weeks). Positive: aerobic exercise ranked highest overall.
items.Effect_Summary: Quantified synthesis: aerobic exercise vs control improved pain short term SMD -1.10 (95% CI -1.68 to -0.52) and mid-term SMD -1.19 (-1.59 to -0.79), function mid-term SMD 1.78 (1.05 to 2.51), gait mid-term SMD 0.85 (0.55 to 1.14), and QoL short term SMD 1.53 (0.47 to 2.59). Safety was reported in 40/217 studies (18%) with no clear between-modality difference.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; level=L2; use=Direct anchor.

items.title: Stationary cycling exercise for knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review and meta-analysis
items.summary: 11 RCTs/724 participants; cycling reduced pain and improved sport performance, while several daily-function domains were mixed or below MCID.
items.why_selected: Supports optional low-impact cycling when walking flares symptoms, while warning not to overclaim all domains.
items.P_Population: 11 RCTs with 724 participants with knee osteoarthritis; final meta-analysis performed with 8 studies.
items.I_Intervention: Stationary cycling exercise, usually low-impact aerobic cycling on upright or recumbent equipment.
items.C_Comparator: No-exercise control or usual care comparator where reported.
items.O_Outcomes: Pain, function, stiffness, symptoms, sport performance, activities of daily living and quality of life. Positive for pain and sport performance; other domains were statistically mixed or below MCID.
items.Effect_Summary: Quantified synthesis: stationary cycling reduced pain versus no-exercise control (WMD 12.86, 95% CI 6.90 to 18.81) and improved sport performance (WMD 8.06, 95% CI 0.92 to 15.20). Stiffness WMD 11.47 (4.69 to 18.25), function WMD 8.28 (2.44 to 14.11), symptoms WMD 4.15 (-1.87 to 10.18), ADL WMD 6.43 (3.19 to 9.66) and QoL WMD 0.99 (-4.27 to 6.25) did not consistently exceed MCID.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; level=L2; use=Direct anchor.

items.title: Strength or aerobic exercise in knee OA: multi-arm randomized controlled trial with 1-year follow-up
items.summary: 168 adults aged 35-70; 12-week supervised strength or stationary cycling did not significantly improve 1-year KOOS QoL versus usual care, but improved strength and VO2max at 4 months.
items.why_selected: Calibrates expectations and supervision for strength/cycling modules in active rehabilitation cases.
items.P_Population: 168 adults aged 35-70 years with symptomatic and radiographic knee osteoarthritis in a three-arm primary-care RCT.
items.I_Intervention: 12 weeks of physiotherapist-supervised strength exercise or aerobic exercise using stationary cycling.
items.O_Outcomes: Primary: KOOS quality of life at 1 year. Secondary: self-reported function, pain, self-efficacy, quadriceps strength and VO2max at 4 months and 1 year.
items.Effect_Summary: RCT signal: no statistically significant KOOS QoL benefit at 1 year versus usual care for strength exercise (mean difference 6.5, 95% CI -0.9 to 14) or aerobic exercise (5.0, 95% CI -2.7 to 12.8). Both exercise groups improved quadriceps strength and VO2max at 4 months versus usual care.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; level=L3; use=Trial calibration.

items.title: Comparison of aerobic modes combined with resistance training in knee OA: randomized controlled trial
items.summary: RCT signal for combining aerobic modes with resistance training over 8 weeks; use for modality choice when walking, cycling or other aerobic options must be adapted.
items.why_selected: Supports modular exercise selection rather than a single generic exercise label.
items.P_Population: Adults with knee osteoarthritis in an 8-week exercise RCT; detailed age/BMI/sample metadata not fully encoded in the local evidence unit.
items.I_Intervention: Different aerobic modes combined with resistance training.
items.C_Comparator: Alternative aerobic modes plus resistance training.
items.O_Outcomes: Pain, function, KOOS, VAS, chair stand, 6-minute walk and TUG; local metadata classifies the signal as positive.
items.Effect_Summary: Trial-level modality support is present, but exact effect sizes are not in the local metadata row; use only as patient-fit support unless source is opened.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; level=L3; use=Trial calibration.

items.title: Evaluating the diagnostic accuracy of a screening tool for low physical activity in independently ambulating adults with knee osteoarthritis: A prosp...
items.summary: Risk/prognosis modifier: use for phenotype, risk and treatment prioritisation.
items.why_selected: Observational evidence is used to phenotype activity, fall risk and function; it is not the primary prescription anchor.
items.patient_fit: Observational evidence is used to phenotype activity, fall risk and function; it is not the primary prescription anchor.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; evidence level=L4; use=Context / support only. Direct prescription decisions remain anchored to L1-L3 evidence and current safety gates.

items.title: The relationship between knee flexion excursion and mechanical stress during gait in medial knee osteoarthritis
items.summary: Supportive evidence: retain for routing, but require higher-tier confirmation before prescription.
items.why_selected: Implementation evidence supports gait, adherence and clinic-delivery details for this high-burden patient.
items.patient_fit: Implementation evidence supports gait, adherence and clinic-delivery details for this high-burden patient.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; evidence level=L5; use=Context / support only. Direct prescription decisions remain anchored to L1-L3 evidence and current safety gates.
items.full_evidence_level: L5 Other original or unclear design

items.title: Eight weeks of a lower limb resistance training protocol and gait performance in patients with symptomatic mild to moderate knee osteoarthritis
items.summary: Protocol/background only: do not use as outcome evidence unless supported by higher tiers.
items.why_selected: Protocol evidence supports safe staged rehabilitation design and stop-rule monitoring.
items.patient_fit: Protocol evidence supports safe staged rehabilitation design and stop-rule monitoring.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; evidence level=L6; use=Background only. Direct prescription decisions remain anchored to L1-L3 evidence and current safety gates.

items.title: Beneficial Effects and Potential Mechanisms of Tai Chi on Lower Limb Osteoarthritis: A Biopsychosocial Perspective
items.summary: Supportive evidence: retain for routing, but require higher-tier confirmation before prescription.
items.why_selected: Background evidence is retained only to explain patient experience and fall-related context.
items.patient_fit: Background evidence is retained only to explain patient experience and fall-related context.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=exercise rehabilitation; evidence level=L7; use=Background only. Direct prescription decisions remain anchored to L1-L3 evidence and current safety gates.

exercise_rehabilitation.freshness_note: Direct recommendations prioritize L1-L3 current anchors; L4-L7 nodes are shown to make patient-fit context, implementation limits and background assumptions reviewable.

nutrition_weight_management.title: Nutrition and weight-management evidence chain
nutrition_weight_management.clinical_question: How should weight reduction be combined with muscle preservation in an older patient with BMI 29.4 and unknown renal function?

items.title: NICE NG226 osteoarthritis guideline
items.summary: Supports weight management and non-pharmacological core treatment in OA.
items.why_selected: Current broad guideline anchor for weight management.
items.patient_fit: Current broad guideline anchor for weight management.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=nutrition weight management; level=L1; use=Direct anchor.

items.title: Weight loss and function outcomes in knee osteoarthritis
items.summary: Supports clinically meaningful weight reduction while monitoring muscle and function.
items.why_selected: Adds outcome evidence for weight targets.
items.patient_fit: Adds outcome evidence for weight targets.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=nutrition weight management; level=L2; use=Direct anchor.

items.title: Once-weekly semaglutide in persons with obesity and knee osteoarthritis
items.summary: STEP 9 RCT: semaglutide 2.4 mg weekly plus lifestyle reduced body weight and WOMAC pain more than placebo in adults with obesity and moderate knee OA.
items.why_selected: Adds a special-population pharmacologic obesity pathway when BMI/obesity indication fits the patient.
items.P_Population: 407 adults with obesity and moderate knee osteoarthritis in a 68-week double-blind randomized trial; BMI obesity indication; Population detail: symptomatic moderate knee OA with obesity; sample/evidence set: 407; extraction status: DOI/PubMed verified targeted abstraction.
items.I_Intervention: semaglutide 2.4 mg once weekly subcutaneous injection plus lifestyle intervention
items.O_Outcomes: body-weight percent change, WOMAC pain score, and physical function; Signal direction: positive signal.
items.Effect_Summary: Targeted quantitative abstraction: at week 68, body weight changed -13.7% with semaglutide vs -3.2% with placebo; WOMAC pain changed -41.7 vs -27.5 points; between-group differences were statistically significant (P<0.001).
items.Safety_or_Contraindication_Note: Use as obesity pharmacotherapy, not as an autonomous OA analgesic; review pregnancy, pancreatitis/gallbladder disease, MEN2/MTC contraindication, GI adverse effects, cost/coverage and continuation plan.
items.Prescription_Use: B: special-population obesity-treatment option for BMI/obesity-eligible knee OA patients; requires prescribing clinician review and lifestyle co-intervention.
items.validation: Targeted source abstraction: NEJM/PubMed verified; use as obesity-treatment module with clinician prescribing boundary.
items.directness: Direct supporting evidence for selected obese knee OA phenotype

items.title: Diet plus exercise for obese adults with knee osteoarthritis
items.summary: Supports combined diet and strengthening rather than diet alone.
items.why_selected: Matches BMI and strength-decline phenotype.
items.patient_fit: Matches BMI and strength-decline phenotype.
items.validation: Patient-fit check: domain=nutrition weight management; level=L3; use=Direct anchor.

agents.name: Exercise and rehabilitation agent
agents.specialty_rule: Output must use complete FITT-VP and then classify options by evidence result tier: guideline core, meta-analysis/synthesis signal, RCT/modality signal, patient preference and safety stop rules.
reasoning: Left KL4 and NRS8 require low-impact loading and flare rules.
Right KL2 still requires strengthening and progression monitoring.
Moderate fall risk requires balance practice and environmental safety.
Quadriceps weakness requires progressive resistance training on both sides with side-specific tolerance.

content: Flat walking, treadmill or indoor route
Start 50-90 min/week, progress toward 150 min/week if stable
Increase duration first, then pace; reduce 24-48 h after flare
Upright or recumbent bike adjusted to avoid deep flexion pain
Progress minutes before resistance; stop for swelling or night-pain increase
Quadriceps, hip abductors, hamstrings, calf, sit-to-stand
Add one set or small load only if next-day pain/function stable
Supported, low fear, no unsafe instability
Tandem stance, supported single-leg stance, step control, gait drills
Remove hand support gradually; supervise if stance <10 s
Use when land walking flares; avoid if wound/infection risk
stop_rules: Pain >5/10 during exercise or worse function next day
Chest pain, unexplained dyspnea or dizziness

result_tiers.message: Therapeutic exercise is offered to all OA patients and tailored to need: local strengthening plus general aerobic fitness.
Progressive strengthening, low-impact aerobic training and supervised programs are prioritized when pain/function burden or adherence risk is present.
Stationary cycling, aquatic exercise and neuromotor/balance training are surfaced as optional modules for patients who flare with walking, fear falling or need low-impact progression.
KL grade is fixed; progression changes load, duration, supervision and modality rather than pretending structure can be adjusted by a slider.

agents.name: Weight, nutrition and metabolism agent
agents.specialty_rule: Nutrition target must be explicit: 5-10% weight reduction when BMI is elevated, paired with muscle preservation, resistance training and renal-function gating before fixed protein targets.
reasoning: BMI 29.4 supports an initial 5% weight-loss target.
Age 67 and bilateral knee symptoms require muscle preservation rather than crash dieting.
Unknown renal function prevents fixed high-protein dosing until eGFR is known.
Nutrition target is tied to pain, walking tolerance, waist/weight trend and strength monitoring.

prescription.target: Nutrition target: reduce baseline weight by 5% over 3-6 months when BMI is elevated; consider 5-10% only if tolerated, strength is preserved and renal/metabolic review is acceptable.
prescription.monitoring: Track body weight weekly, waist monthly, left/right pain and walking tolerance every 2-4 weeks, and strength/function at 6-12 weeks.
prescription.protein_boundary: After eGFR review, distribute protein across meals to support resistance training; avoid fixed high-protein dosing while renal function is unknown.
prescription.plate: Half non-starchy vegetables, one quarter lean protein after renal review, one quarter whole grains or starchy vegetables, with unsaturated fat in small amounts.
eat_more: Leafy greens, broccoli, peppers, tomatoes, mushrooms, cucumber and carrots
Legumes and soy foods: beans, lentils, chickpeas, tofu or edamame
Fish, skinless poultry, eggs or low-fat dairy if tolerated and appropriate after renal review
Whole grains or starchy vegetables in controlled portions: oats, brown rice, whole-wheat noodles, corn, potato or sweet potato
Nuts or olive/rapeseed oil in small portions
sample_day: Breakfast: oats or whole-grain staple plus egg or yogurt and fruit.
Lunch: two vegetables, tofu/fish/chicken or legumes, and a controlled whole-grain portion.
Dinner: vegetable-forward meal with protein, no sugary drink, and smaller late-night starch portion.
Exercise days: distribute protein across meals after renal review and pair nutrition with resistance training.

result_tiers.message: Weight management is offered when BMI is elevated; any weight loss may help, and larger targets can help more if muscle is preserved.
For BMI/obesity-eligible knee OA patients, STEP 9 supports considering semaglutide 2.4 mg weekly as obesity pharmacotherapy with lifestyle intervention and clinician prescribing boundaries.
CKD, frailty or malnutrition risk changes protein targets and requires renal/metabolic review before fixed dosing.
Food examples are made selectable so the clinician can prescribe practical patient-preferred options, not only abstract categories.

agents.name: Medication and injection agent
agents.specialty_rule: Medication output must name specific medicines, dose/frequency, duration, contraindication gates and monitoring; oral NSAID is deferred or conditional until renal, GI, anticoagulant/current medication and cardiovascular review is complete.
reasoning: High left-knee pain requires analgesic support.
Right-knee symptoms are milder and should not receive escalation copied from the left knee.
Missing safety data prevents routine oral NSAID.
Avoiding repeated injections downgrades routine injection.

safety_block: GI ulcer or bleeding history: MISSING
items: Diclofenac 1% gel: apply 4 g to the painful knee up to four times daily for 2-4 weeks; do not exceed local label limits, avoid broken skin and monitor rash or irritation.

Celecoxib 100 mg twice daily or 200 mg once daily may be considered only after clinician review of eGFR, GI bleeding risk, anticoagulant/antiplatelet status, current medications and cardiovascular risk; use the lowest effective dose for the shortest duration.
Naproxen 250 mg twice daily is an alternative oral NSAID after the same safety review; do not combine with another oral NSAID and consider gastroprotection when risk requires it.
Ibuprofen 400 mg every 6-8 hours as needed is a short-course option only after safety review; avoid combining with aspirin, anticoagulants or other NSAIDs without clinician direction.
Acetaminophen/paracetamol 500-1000 mg every 6-8 hours as needed can be used for short-term episodic pain within local daily dose limits; reduce limits with liver disease, alcohol misuse or frailty.

Semaglutide 2.4 mg once weekly is a special-population obesity-treatment option for BMI/obesity-eligible knee OA patients after clinician/endocrine review; STEP 9 supports weight and WOMAC pain improvement, but it is not an autonomous OA analgesic.
Intra-articular triamcinolone acetonide 40 mg may be considered for the symptomatic knee when flare, effusion or pain blocks rehabilitation; avoid routine repeated injections and reassess response.

Sodium hyaluronate / hyaluronic acid injection is not a routine default: use only as a documented shared-decision option when standard treatments are unsuitable and uncertainty, cost and local product schedule are explained.
Platelet-rich plasma (PRP) is not a routine default: if offered locally, document preparation, injection number/interval, cost, uncertainty and consent; avoid structural-regeneration claims.

result_tiers.message: Topical NSAID is first-line for knee OA pain when skin is intact; oral NSAID requires renal, GI, anticoagulant/current-medication and CV review.
Celecoxib, naproxen or ibuprofen are named options only after the gate is complete and should use lowest effective dose for shortest duration.
Corticosteroid injection is a short-term bridge when pain/effusion blocks rehabilitation; sodium hyaluronate and PRP are not routine defaults and require shared-decision documentation.
Semaglutide is surfaced only for obesity-indicated patients as a metabolic treatment option, using STEP 9 as RCT support and retaining label contraindication and prescribing review.

agents.name: Psychology, behavior and adherence agent
agents.specialty_rule: Screen anxiety, depression, pain catastrophizing and sleep; if positive, provide guideline-consistent stepped psychological intervention with CBT, behavioral activation, pacing, relaxation and referral boundaries.
reasoning: High pain and high demand require expectation management.
GAD-7, PHQ-9 and PCS results decide intensity of psychological support.
Behavior support improves adherence to rehabilitation and weight goals.
Self-harm, severe depression or uncontrolled anxiety requires urgent specialist pathway rather than routine OA coaching.

screening: Self-harm question when PHQ-9 item 9 or clinical concern is positive
intervention: If GAD-7 or PHQ-9 is mild: guided self-help using CBT principles, activity scheduling, sleep routine and weekly symptom tracking for 4-6 weeks.
If anxiety/depression is moderate or persistent: refer for structured CBT, problem-solving therapy, behavioral activation or applied relaxation while continuing OA rehabilitation.
Use neutral pain neuroscience education: pain is influenced by joint structure, load, inflammation, sleep, stress and nervous-system sensitivity; it is not imagined pain.
Conversation cue: acknowledge pain, explain that joint structure, load, sleep, stress and nervous-system sensitivity interact, then agree on one two-week goal.
Use activity pacing: split the 3 km goal into tolerated walking intervals, alternate activity and rest before flare, and progress only after both knees remain stable.
Teach a flare script: diaphragmatic breathing 5 minutes, progressive muscle relaxation 10 minutes, reduce load for 24-48 hours, then resume graded movement.
Behavioral activation: schedule one meaningful low-load activity each day and track completion, mood, sleep and pain interference.
Escalate urgently to mental-health services if self-harm signal, severe depression, severe insomnia, panic preventing care, substance misuse or inability to adhere safely is present.

result_tiers.message: GAD-7, PHQ-9, pain catastrophizing and sleep signals are read from KOM-Profile before advice is generated.
Mild/moderate anxiety, depression, insomnia or catastrophizing triggers CBT-informed explanation, behavioral activation, relaxation, pacing and referral boundaries.
If no screen is recorded, the output must state that the screen is missing and use only low-risk education until screening is completed.

agents.name: Orthopedic surgery recommendation and preoperative warning agent
agents.specialty_rule: The system may recommend orthopedic specialist evaluation when side-specific KL3-4 disease, severe pain/function loss and insufficient conservative response are present; it records reasons and preoperative warnings but does not choose TKA, UKA or HTO.
reasoning: Left KL4, NRS8 and WOMAC62 indicate high burden.
Right KL2 should not receive the same surgical recommendation as the left knee.
Persistent severe symptoms plus high walking goal justify specialist evaluation rather than indefinite conservative-only care.
Surgery type and timing require orthopedic specialist assessment.

prescription.decision: recommend orthopedic specialist evaluation for the left knee because KL4 structural OA, severe pain, major function limitation and high walking demand are present. Continue conservative monitoring for the right knee unless side-specific worsening occurs. The system does not decide TKA, UKA or HTO.
why_recommended: Left knee KL4 with severe medial JSN, osteophytes and sclerosis
Pain NRS 8 and WOMAC function 62 indicate high symptomatic burden
Patient goal requires reliable walking capacity
Conservative-treatment history is incomplete and must be documented before surgical decision-making
preoperative_warning: Updated weight-bearing AP/lateral radiographs and long-leg alignment status are required for referral screening.
Record knee ROM, flexion contracture, effusion and gait-aid requirement.
Record conservative-treatment duration, adherence and response before referral is framed as appropriate.
Collect self-reported cardiovascular warning symptoms, respiratory risk, anticoagulant/antiplatelet use, diabetes control signal, skin wounds and dental/infection signal.
This is a pre-referral screen, not a final surgical admission workup or procedure selection.

result_tiers.message: Advanced side-specific KL3-4 disease plus severe pain/function loss supports orthopedic specialist evaluation, not automatic surgery selection.

Weight-bearing radiographs/alignment status, ROM, conservative-treatment response, self-reported CV/respiratory risk, infection screen, anticoagulant/diabetes flags must be recorded or flagged missing.
The AI can recommend referral and prehabilitation; it does not choose TKA, UKA or HTO.

safety.finding: Left and right knees have different KL grades, symptoms and treatment implications.
safety.decision: Every prediction, imaging interpretation and treatment recommendation must state the knee side.

safety.finding: Renal, GI, anticoagulant/current medication and cardiovascular review are incomplete.
safety.decision: Route back to medication agent; oral NSAID remains deferred or strictly conditional.

safety.finding: Patient wants to avoid repeated injections.
safety.decision: Injection may only be discussed as short-term bridge therapy when flare, effusion or rehabilitation-blocking pain is present.

safety.finding: BMI is elevated and strength must be preserved.
safety.decision: Use explicit 5% weight target over 3-6 months, paired with resistance training and renal review before fixed protein dosing.

safety.finding: High pain/high demand requires screening for anxiety, depression, catastrophizing and sleep disturbance.
safety.decision: Positive screens trigger CBT-informed self-help, behavioral activation, relaxation, pacing and referral boundaries.

safety.gate: Orthopedic recommendation and preoperative warning gate
safety.finding: Left KL4 and severe symptoms support orthopedic specialist evaluation.
safety.decision: Recommend specialist evaluation with reasons and preoperative warning checklist; the system does not choose TKA/UKA/HTO.

report: Bilateral knee OA profile: left knee KL4 with severe pain/function burden; right knee KL2 with milder symptoms and active monitoring needs. Medication safety data remain incomplete and must be reviewed before routine oral NSAID escalation.

Use side-specific rehabilitation, explicit Nutrition target, concrete topical/oral medication options with dose/frequency gates, CBT-informed psychology intervention after screening, and recommend orthopedic specialist evaluation for the left knee with preoperative warning review.

Evidence selection prioritizes current guidelines, meta-analysis/systematic review evidence and RCT/clinical study detail. KOM-RAG patient-fit retrieval succeeds only after L1, L2 and L3 evidence are returned; KOM-Safe routes non-pass gates back to the responsible specialty agent.

Bilateral knee identity, medication safety, injection indication, nutrition target, anxiety/depression intervention, referral readiness, preoperative warning status and missing information completion. This system supports clinical decision-making but does not replace clinician judgment.

metrics: Ablation across left/right KL and symptom anchors

trace.artifact: Editable patient profile and selected case assessment.
Original image, annotation and structured radiographic findings.
Three endpoint risk estimates and sensitivity analysis.
Guideline, meta-analysis, RCT and context evidence chain.
Gate audit, specialty revision and re-audit.
Assessment report, treatment prescription and reasoning trail.

institutional_context: West China Hospital Sports Medicine clinical research platform

clinical_score_set.name: WOMAC
clinical_score_set.use: Pain, stiffness and physical function in knee or hip osteoarthritis.

clinical_score_set.name: KOOS
clinical_score_set.use: Symptoms, pain, activities of daily living, sport/recreation and knee-related quality of life.

clinical_score_set.name: Oxford Knee Score
clinical_score_set.use: Twelve-item knee pain and function measure, commonly used around surgical evaluation.

clinical_score_set.name: MRC strength
clinical_score_set.use: Manual muscle testing from 0 to 5, paired with dynamometer strength when available.

clinical_score_set.name: TUG / chair stand / 6-minute walk
clinical_score_set.use: Performance measures for mobility, lower-limb function and fall-risk context.
